input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt:
Given inputs question (string) and passages (list of strings), produce a single textual output to populate the field "summary". The "summary" output must include the following labeled sections and follow these rules exactly.

1) Format
- Begin with: [[ ## reasoning ## ]]
- End the reasoning block and then include: [[ ## summary ## ]]
- The final output may optionally end with: [[ ## completed ## ]]

2) Reasoning section (required)
- For each passage i, include a concise mapping that:
  - Quotes or paraphrases the exact supporting text from Passage [i] (use quotation marks or exact substring).
  - Explicitly maps each quoted fact to the entity and scope it supports (e.g., "Passage [3] → ENTITY: Dan Boren; SCOPE: biographical birth date"; or "Passage [1] → ENTITY: Ilha (Santana); SCOPE: parish-level population 2011").
  - If a passage contains only lower-level, local, or different-scope facts that are NOT sufficient to answer the question (e.g., parish populations when the question asks for archipelago total), explicitly state that and refuse to treat them as the final answer.

- After mapping passages, state whether the exact target fact required by the question is present in the provided passages:
  - If present: identify the exact sentence(s) and supporting-text pattern matched (literal pattern required, e.g., "ENTITY_NAME (born <date>)" or "The population in 2011 was <number>"). Cite passage indices that contain that exact supporting text.
  - If not present: explain precisely which fact is missing and why the existing passage facts are insufficient (cite passage indices).

3) If missing: prioritized next-hop retrieval plan (required when exact supporting text is absent)
- Name the target entity to retrieve (canonical/title-first form) and give one-sentence rationale linking it to the question (use the bridging passage index).
- Provide at least three disambiguating short query variants (each a short search phrase). At least one variant must begin with the canonical/title-first suggestion. Include scope/context tokens (e.g., "birth date", "2011 population", "archipelago", "U.S. Representative", "politician", location).
- Specify the exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document that will permit a final answer (literal pattern, e.g., "Dan Boren (born <date>)", or "The population of Madeira in 2011 was <number>", or "X (born YYYY-MM-DD)").
- Provide the exact bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include to connect current passages to the target (e.g., "Dan Boren" + "Oklahoma's 2nd Congressional District" or "Ilha (Santana) → Madeira").
- Include a short reasoning subsection that cites the passage indices showing why a second hop is needed (one or two sentences).

4) If present: final-answer extraction
- Extract the final answer verbatim from the supporting sentence(s) in the passages.
- State the exact supporting-text pattern matched.
- Cite the passage index(es) that contain the supporting text.

5) Summary section (one line)
- Provide a one-line summary that is either:
  - The cited answer followed by its supporting passage index(es), OR
  - A concise retrieval plan statement that names the target entity and says "retrieve [target]" (and optionally lists the top query variant), if a second hop is needed.
- Do not include any new facts not present in the passages or the retrieval plan.

6) Constraints and behavior
- Never answer from memory. If the exact required supporting text is not in the provided passages, do not invent or guess an answer—emit the retrieval plan described above.
- Be concise and prioritized: when providing query variants, order them by priority.
- Always include passage indices in reasoning to justify conclusions.
- Generalize this behavior to any question type (dates, populations, roles, totals, comparisons, etc.).

delta_p.trigger: When the question requests a person who is/was a member of a named band (e.g., 'former member of the Eagles') and the provided passages attribute the work (song/album) to a named artist but do not yet contain an explicit, literal statement confirming that artist's membership in the band named in the question.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Preserve all existing formatting and mapping behavior required by the base prompt: produce the [[ ## reasoning ## ]] block, include for each passage a concise quoted or paraphrased supporting-text mapping with explicit 'Passage [i] → ENTITY: ...; SCOPE: ...' lines, and include the [[ ## summary ## ]] block and one-line summary as currently specified.
delta_p.change_rule: After producing the per-passage mappings, perform this conditional check and branch: (A) If any passage contains an exact supporting-text pattern that unambiguously states the artist's membership in the band named in the question (literal patterns such as '<Name>, the lead vocalist and drummer for the Eagles' or '<Name>, former member of <Band>' or '<Name> (born ...) — [role] in <Band>'), then follow the base prompt's 'final-answer extraction' behavior: extract the final answer verbatim from that sentence, state the exact supporting-text pattern matched, and cite the passage index(es). (B) If no passage contains such an explicit literal statement, do NOT guess or answer from memory. Instead emit a prioritized next-hop retrieval plan as required by the base prompt, with these required elements: 1) Name the target entity to retrieve using the canonical/title-first form (use the artist name already found in the passages, e.g., 'Don Henley'). 2) Provide a one-sentence rationale linking that target to the question and cite the bridging passage index that attributes the work to the artist. 3) List at least three short query variants ordered by priority (the first variant must begin with the canonical/title-first suggestion); include scope/context tokens such as 'biography', 'Eagles membership', 'AllMusic', 'Wikipedia', 'years', 'drummer', 'lead vocalist'. 4) Specify the exact literal supporting-text pattern that must appear in the retrieved document to permit a final answer (e.g., 'Don Henley, .* (lead vocalist|drummer|member) .* Eagles' or 'Don Henley (born YYYY) — .* Eagles' or 'X is/was a member of the Eagles'). 5) Provide the exact bridging phrase(s) the follow-up query should include to connect current passages to the target (for example: 'Don Henley' + 'Eagles' or 'Don Henley' + 'biography'). 6) Include a short reasoning subsection (1–2 sentences) citing the passage index(es) that show why the second hop is needed (e.g., passages that attribute the song to the artist but lack an explicit membership sentence). Finally, ensure the one-line summary names the target entity and says 'retrieve [target]' (and may list the top query variant).
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not invent or infer band membership from general familiarity or partial phrasing; never answer from memory. Do not produce long verbatim spans that exist only in external targets. Do not change the overall output format or omit required passage-index citations. Avoid adding unrelated retrieval goals or extra facts not present in the passages or the retrieval plan.
delta_p.output_contract: The agent's output must: (1) include the [[ ## reasoning ## ]] block with per-passage quoted/paraphrased mappings and explicit 'Passage [i] → ENTITY: ...; SCOPE: ...' lines; (2) explicitly state whether an exact literal supporting sentence that proves the artist's membership in the named band is present, and cite passage index(es); (3a) if such a literal sentence is present, extract the final answer verbatim, state the exact supporting-text pattern matched, and cite indices; OR (3b) if no literal sentence is present, emit a prioritized next-hop retrieval plan containing the canonical target entity, one-sentence rationale with bridging passage index, ≥3 ordered query variants (first begins with canonical), the exact required supporting-text pattern, explicit bridging phrase(s), and a 1–2 sentence reasoning subsection citing passages; (4) conclude with the [[ ## summary ## ]] one-line summary that either gives the cited answer plus indices or says 'retrieve [target]' (with optional top query). Do not add facts not supported by passages or the retrieval plan.
delta_p.why_local_transition: Make the agent explicitly check for and surface whether the passages contain an explicit membership statement and, when absent, produce a narrowly focused next-hop retrieval plan (target entity + prioritized queries + exact evidence pattern) rather than prematurely emitting a terse answer.

delta_p.trigger: When the reasoning maps one or more passages to a named person (a bridge entity appears, e.g., 'Don Henley') but the mapped facts do not contain the specific relation or phrasing required by the question (here: explicit membership/association with the Eagles), apply this prompt delta.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the existing output format and all mapping behavior: produce the [[ ## reasoning ## ]] and [[ ## summary ## ]] blocks, quote/paraphrase per passage, map each quoted fact to ENTITY and SCOPE, and state whether the exact target fact is present. Preserve any supporting evidence already cited from the passages.
delta_p.change_rule: When triggered, strengthen the 'If missing' (next-hop retrieval plan) content: explicitly name the retrieval target in canonical/title-first form (use the bridge entity as the target, e.g., 'Don Henley') and state one-sentence rationale linking that target to the question. Then list at least three prioritized short query variants (priority-ordered) with the first variant beginning with the canonical/title-first term (e.g., 'Don Henley ...'). Require the plan to specify the exact literal supporting-text pattern that will permit a final answer (examples: a phrase combining the canonical name and an explicit membership phrase such as "Don Henley" + "member of the Eagles" or a phrase like "Don Henley" + "lead vocalist and drummer for the Eagles"). Also require the plan to supply explicit bridging phrase(s) to include in the follow-up query (e.g., 'Don Henley' + 'Eagles' or 'Don Henley' + 'member'). Keep the retrieval-plan rationale concise and cite the bridging passage index(es).
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not assume or assert the membership relation as an answer when it is not literally present in the passages. Do not produce a final answer from memory. Avoid adding extra target facts (years or other membership details) unless those exact literal patterns appear in retrieved or provided text.
delta_p.output_contract: After applying this delta, when the passages identify a bridge person but lack the exact membership phrasing, the summary must: (a) name the canonical retrieval target (e.g., 'Don Henley'); (b) give a one-sentence rationale linking that target to the question with a citation to the bridging passage index; (c) list ≥3 prioritized short query variants (first begins with the canonical name); (d) state the exact literal supporting-text pattern required to accept a retrieved document as conclusive (literal pattern combining the canonical name and an explicit membership phrase); (e) give explicit bridging phrase(s) for the follow-up query; and (f) include a one- or two-sentence reasoning justification citing the passage index(es). All other formatting and mapping requirements remain unchanged.
delta_p.why_local_transition: This is a small, local clarification: the agent already exposes 'Don Henley' as the bridge entity but the next-hop retrieval instruction in the source was generic. The change tightens the next-hop to a canonical target (Don Henley biography/article) and requires an explicit literal supporting-text pattern for 'member of the Eagles' so the downstream hop can deterministically confirm membership without guessing.

delta_p.trigger: Apply when the question requests an artist's band membership (e.g., 'former member of the Eagles') and the mapped passages attribute the work to an individual but do not contain an explicit statement that the individual is/was a member of the named band.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep all existing reasoning behavior: quote or paraphrase supporting text from each passage, map each quoted fact to ENTITY and SCOPE, explicitly state whether the exact target fact is present, and refuse to answer from memory when the exact supporting text is absent.
delta_p.change_rule: When the trigger applies, append to the required 'If missing: prioritized next-hop retrieval plan' these specific instructions: (a) set the canonical retrieval target to the bridge entity in title-first form (e.g., 'Don Henley') and state a one-sentence rationale linking the bridge passage index (e.g., 'Passage [1] identifies the song artist'). (b) Provide at least three disambiguating short query variants ordered by priority; the first variant MUST begin with the canonical/title-first form (e.g., 'Don Henley membership Eagles', then 'Don Henley biography Eagles member', then 'Don Henley Eagles founding member years'). (c) Specify the exact supporting-text pattern required from the retrieved document to permit a final answer: include one of these literal sentence patterns (any exact match qualifies): 'Don Henley is a member of the Eagles', 'Don Henley was a member of the Eagles', 'Don Henley is a founding member of the Eagles', or 'Don Henley is a former member of the Eagles'. (d) Supply bridging phrase(s) for the follow-up query such as 'Don Henley' + 'Eagles' + 'member' (or 'membership', 'founding member'). (e) In the short reasoning subsection, cite the passage index(es) (e.g., [1],[2]) that show the song/album is attributed to Don Henley and therefore motivate retrieving his biography.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not treat other non-membership facts (release year, chart positions, album credits) as sufficient to answer the membership question; do not answer the membership question from memory; avoid adding extra retrieval targets or unrelated biographical queries (awards, solo discography) unless they directly support membership confirmation.
delta_p.output_contract: When the agent emits a 'If missing: prioritized next-hop retrieval plan' it must: (1) name the canonical target exactly as 'Don Henley' (title-first), (2) give the one-sentence rationale referencing the bridging passage index, (3) list at least three ordered query variants with the first beginning with 'Don Henley', (4) state the exact literal supporting-text pattern required (one of the listed sentence patterns), (5) include bridging phrase(s) to use in the follow-up query, and (6) include a one- or two-sentence reasoning citation giving the passage indices that justify the second hop.
delta_p.why_local_transition: This small change forces the agent to produce a concrete, prioritized retrieval plan that names the correct target entity (Don Henley) and exact supporting-text pattern needed to resolve the missing membership fact, aligning the agent's next-hop output with the pipeline's requirement to fetch a biography page that can supply explicit membership wording.

visible_agent_inputs.question: The End of the Innocence was a single by which former member of the Eagles?
passages: The End of the Innocence (song) | "The End of the Innocence" is the lead single and title track from Don Henley's third solo studio album of the same name, released in 1989.  Henley co-wrote and co-produced the song with Bruce Hornsby, who also performed piano; both artists perform the song live in their respective concerts.  Henley's version peaked at number eight on the "Billboard" Hot 100, becoming his fifth solo top ten hit on the chart, more than any of the other members of The Eagles.  "The End of the Innocence" also became his fourth number-one single on the Mainstream Rock Tracks chart and peaked at number two on the Hot Adult Contemporary chart.  The song features Wayne Shorter on saxophone.

The End of the Innocence (album) | The End of the Innocence is the third solo studio album by Don Henley, the lead vocalist and drummer for the Eagles.  The album was released in 1989, on Geffen Records, and would be his last release on that label.  It was also his last solo album of the 1980s and it would be eleven years before he released another solo album, until 2000's "Inside Job".

Innocent (Stereophonics song) | "Innocent" is a single by British rock band Stereophonics which was released on 9 November 2009.  It was the first single taken from their seventh studio album, "Keep Calm and Carry On", which was released a week later, on 16 November 2009.  It was also the band's first single for Mercury Records.

Sweet and Innocent (Diamond Head song) | Sweet and Innocent is a single by heavy metal band Diamond Head released in 1980 by Media Records, a Wolverhampton-based label.  It was a single A-side "Streets of Gold" as the B-Side, and was only available on 7".  Both tracks eventually ended up on the re-released version of Diamond Head's 1980 debut Lightning to the Nations in 2001 by Sanctuary Records.

Moving Pictures (band) | Moving Pictures were an Australian rock music band formed in 1978.  Their debut album, "Days of Innocence", was issued in October 1981 and eventually peaked at No. 1 on the Kent Music Report Albums Chart in February the following year.  In January 1982 they released their single, "What About Me", which reached No. 1 on the Kent Music Report Singles Chart.  Late that year Elektra Records issued "Days of Innocence" and "What About Me" in North America.  The single reached No. 29 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and appeared on the associated year-end Hot 100 list for 1983.  A proposed series of United States performances supporting REO Speedwagon, Tom Petty and Hall & Oates fell through when Elektra was substantially reorganised.

The Last Worthless Evening | "The Last Worthless Evening" is a song written by John Corey, Don Henley, and Stan Lynch.  It was a single recorded by Henley in 1989 which reached #21 on US charts.  The song was included on Henley's third album "The End of the Innocence" that same year.
Innocent Chinyoka | Innocent Chinyoka (born 21 June 1982) is a Zimbabwean first-class cricketer who plays for Mashonaland Eagles.

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_1 string. Summarize the visible hop1 passages and expose a useful second-hop retrieval direction.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are executing one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Use the unchanged base instruction p together with the complete ORDERED
list of local prompt deltas. The deltas are supplemental behavior guidance
for this sample; they are not outputs and must not be quoted mechanically.

This is a direct delta-context execution probe:
- Do not rewrite, aggregate, or synthesize a new prompt p'.
- Apply every delta in order to the visible inputs.
- Judge success only by producing the endpoint agent output.
- Do not attempt to reproduce or discuss intermediate states.
- Do not infer hidden information from the existence of the deltas.
- Use only the visible agent inputs as factual evidence.
- Never output gold/oracle labels, evaluation metrics, or hidden support.
- Preserve the agent's normal input/output contract.

Agent:
summary1

Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
